title: حماية الأطفال عبر المحتوى الرقمي المختار

Be aware that other people online may give false information to convince you to share your private information.” Service providers can partner with organizations that are well- positioned to educate and support children on safer internet usage and related issues. See Child Helpline International and GSMA practical guide for Child Helplines and Mobile Operators: Working together to protect children’s rights for examples. Promoting digital technology as a Refer to the general guidelines in Table 1. mode to further civic 5.2 Feature B: Offer curated digital content The Internet provides all types of content and activities, many of which are intended for children and young people. Services offering editorially curated content have tremendous opportunities to build safety and privacy into their offers for children and young people. This service feature addresses both businesses that are creating their own content, and those that are enabling access to digital content. It refers to, inter alia, news and multimedia streaming services, national and public service broadcasting and the gaming industry. Table 3 provides guidance for providers of services offering editorially curated content on policies and actions they can take to enhance child online protection and participation. Guidelines for industry on Child Online Protection Table 3: COP checklist for Feature B: Offer curated digital content Integrating child rights Services providing curated digital content can help identify, considerations into all prevent and mitigate adverse impacts of ICTs on children and appropriate corporate young people’s rights, and identify opportunities to support
text_simplified: يجب على الأهل تعليم أطفالهم عدم مشاركة معلومات الحساب الشخصي مثل كلمة المرور أو اسم المستخدم مع أي شخص عبر الإنترنت. يمكن للمحتوى الرقمي المختار بعناية أن يساعد في حماية الأطفال وتعزيز مشاركتهم الإيجابية.
